mandate.item1: Receive all public monies and make disbursements
mandate.item2: Open and operate bank accounts for the Government of Ghana
mandate.item3: Maintain proper records and books of accounts for Government transactions
mandate.item4: Prepare and submit consolidated financial statements within 3 months of the end of each financial year
mandate.item5: Exercise supervision over the receipt, custody, and disbursement of public monies
mandate.item6: Inspect the accounts of all heads of departments and all persons entrusted with collection, receipt, custody, issue or payment of public monies
mandate.item7: Examine and audit all Government payments
mandate.item8: Bring to the notice of the Auditor-General any irregularity disclosed by the accounts
mandate.item9: Take charge of all government stores and verify the accuracy of the government stores and stock records
mandate.item10: Train and develop public sector accounting personnel
mandate.item11: Process and manage the government payroll

values.customersFirstDesc: We exist to serve. Every decision is guided by how it impacts the citizens and institutions who depend on our services.
values.servingCountryDesc: Our mandate spans all 16 regions and 703+ MDAs. We bring consistent, equitable financial management to every corner of Ghana.
values.integrityDesc: Transparency and honesty are non-negotiable. We uphold the highest ethical standards in managing public funds.
values.valuingPeopleDesc: Our staff are our greatest asset. We invest in their growth and empower them to deliver excellence.
values.innovationDesc: From manual ledgers to GIFMIS and electronic payments — we continuously evolve to serve Ghana better.

whoWeAre.title: Who We Are
whoWeAre.description: Learn about the Controller & Accountant-General's Department — Ghana's premier public financial management institution since 1885.
whoWeAre.timelineDesc: Over 140 years of safeguarding Ghana's public finances
whoWeAre.aboutPara1: The Controller & Accountant-General's Department (CAGD) is Ghana's premier public financial management institution, established in 1885. As the Chief Accounting Officer of Government, the Controller & Accountant-General is responsible for managing all government financial transactions.
whoWeAre.aboutPara2: Operating under the Public Financial Management Act, 2016 (Act 921), CAGD ensures the efficient collection, custody, and disbursement of all government revenues and public funds across 703+ Ministries, Departments, and Agencies (MDAs).
whoWeAre.aboutPara3: The Department is headed by the Controller & Accountant-General, who is ably assisted by six Deputy Controllers & Accountant-Generals, each heading a specialised division. With a presence in all 16 regions of Ghana, CAGD delivers consistent, equitable financial management services nationwide.
whoWeAre.aboutPara4: CAGD has achieved significant milestones including the adoption of 31 of 36 IPSAS standards, production of Ghana's first whole-of-government financial accounts, full transition to Electronic Fund Transfers, and integration with the National Identification Authority for biometric payroll validation.

missionVision.title: Mission & Vision
missionVision.description: Guiding principles that drive CAGD's commitment to public financial management excellence.
missionVision.missionStatement: To provide efficient public financial management services through skilled and motivated staff, leveraging technology and innovation.
missionVision.visionStatement: To be recognized as the best institution providing Public Financial Management services in Africa.
missionVision.howWeDeliverDesc: Four strategic pillars that enable us to achieve our mission
missionVision.efficiencyDesc: Streamlined processes ensuring timely service delivery
missionVision.techDrivenDesc: Leveraging modern systems like GIFMIS and EFT
missionVision.innovationPillarDesc: Adapting global best practices for local impact
missionVision.achievementTitle: Leading Public Financial Management in West Africa
missionVision.achievementDesc: Ghana's Controller & Accountant-General's Department has set the standard for transparent, accountable public finance management across the African continent.
missionVision.achievement1: First in West Africa to produce whole-of-government financial reports
missionVision.achievement2: Joined elite group: UK, Canada, Sweden, Australia, New Zealand
missionVision.achievement3: Aligned with IMF Government Finance Statistics standards
missionVision.achievement4: Real-time biometric payroll validation with NIA
missionVision.achievement5: Full Electronic Fund Transfer for all government payments
missionVision.achievement6: 31 of 36 IPSAS standards adopted and implemented

coreFunctions.title: Core Functions
coreFunctions.description: Eight core functions mandated by the Public Financial Management Act, 2016 (Act 921).
coreFunctions.introText: The Controller and Accountant-General's Department is mandated by the Public Financial Management Act, 2016 (Act 921) to perform the following core functions:
coreFunctions.revenueCollectionDesc: Receive all public monies collected by or payable to the Government and manage their proper accounting and recording across all MDAs.
coreFunctions.secureCustodyDesc: Ensure safe keeping of all public monies, securities, and other financial instruments belonging to the Government of Ghana.
coreFunctions.disbursementsDesc: Process and execute all government payments including salaries, pensions, grants, and supplier payments through established financial systems.
coreFunctions.accountEstablishmentDesc: Open and operate bank accounts for the Government of Ghana with the Bank of Ghana and other approved financial institutions.
coreFunctions.bankingAuthorityDesc: Maintain exclusive authority over all government banking relationships, ensuring proper management of public funds in the banking system.
coreFunctions.financialReportingDesc: Prepare and submit consolidated financial statements of the government within three months of the end of each financial year.
coreFunctions.accountingStandardsDesc: Develop and implement accounting standards, policies, and procedures for all government entities and public sector organizations.
coreFunctions.systemsDevelopmentDesc: Design, develop, and maintain financial management information systems including GIFMIS, payroll systems, and electronic payment platforms.
coreFunctions.revenueCollectionFull: Done through its staff stationed in all government departments including the revenue collecting ones.
coreFunctions.secureCustodyFull: With the support of the Ministry of Finance and Bank of Ghana, ensuring the safety of all public funds.
coreFunctions.disbursementsFull: Including monthly salaries to government employees, pension gratuity and monthly pension payments to retirees, and releases of funds for government projects and development throughout the country.
coreFunctions.accountEstablishmentFull: Establish, on behalf of government, such accounts with the Bank of Ghana and its agents for the deposit of Public and Trust monies.
coreFunctions.bankingAuthorityFull: Be solely responsible for the opening of bank accounts for any government department nationwide.
coreFunctions.financialReportingFull: Keep, prepare, render and publish Financial Statements on the Consolidated Fund of Ghana monthly and annually, not later than three months after the end of the financial year. The Auditor-General is furnished with the necessary financial statements for scrutiny and onward transmission to Parliament.
coreFunctions.accountingStandardsFull: Ensuring all government departments follow consistent, standardized accounting practices aligned with IPSAS.
coreFunctions.systemsDevelopmentFull: Promote the development of efficient accounting systems in all Government Departments through technology, innovation, and digital transformation.
coreFunctions.legalPara1: These core functions are enshrined in Ghana's 1992 Constitution and further detailed in the Public Financial Management Act, 2016 (Act 921).
coreFunctions.legalPara2: The PFM Act strengthened CAGD's mandate by clearly defining its controller role alongside its traditional accounting functions, giving the department the legal authority to oversee all government financial transactions and ensure proper accountability.
coreFunctions.legalPara3: Under this framework, CAGD serves as the central hub for all government financial management, working closely with the Ministry of Finance, Bank of Ghana, Ghana Revenue Authority, and the Auditor-General.

ourHistory.title: Our History
ourHistory.description: From 'The Treasury' in 1885 to a modern digital financial management powerhouse — over 140 years of service to Ghana.
ourHistory.timelineDesc: Tracing the evolution of Ghana's premier financial management institution
ourHistory.journeySummary: Over 140 years of safeguarding Ghana's public finances
ourHistory.journeySubtext: From colonial treasury to a digital-first, internationally recognized institution
ourHistory.cagLeadersDesc: The leaders who have guided CAGD
ourHistory.era1Event1: The British colonial administration establishes 'The Treasury' in the Gold Coast to manage public revenue and expenditure.
ourHistory.era1Event2: The Treasury handles all colonial government receipts and payments across the Gold Coast territories.
ourHistory.era1Event3: Expansion of financial management functions across Gold Coast territories as colonial administration grows.
ourHistory.era2Event1: The Treasury is re-christened to the Accountant-General's Department, reflecting its expanded accounting and financial management responsibilities.
ourHistory.era2Event2: Financial systems modernized during and after World War II to meet increased demands.
ourHistory.era2Event3: Preparations for self-governance bring new financial management responsibilities and institutional reform.
ourHistory.era3Event1: Ghana gains independence on March 6th. The department serves the new Republic of Ghana.
ourHistory.era3Event2: Established under the Civil Service Act 1960 (CA. 5) as the Accounting Class of the Civil Service.
ourHistory.era3Event3: Rapid expansion of government services requires enhanced financial oversight and accountability.
ourHistory.era4Event1: Renamed to the Controller & Accountant-General's Department (CAGD), clearly defining its expanded role in financial controls and budget execution.
ourHistory.era4Event2: Economic reforms and structural adjustment programmes reshape public financial management across Ghana.
ourHistory.era5Event1: The Fourth Republic's 1992 Constitution formally enshrines CAGD's role in public financial management.
ourHistory.era5Event2: Internal Audit Agency Act 2003 (Act 658) strengthens internal audit and oversight mechanisms.
ourHistory.era5Event3: Ghana commits to accrual-based IPSAS adoption — a landmark decision for financial transparency.
ourHistory.era6Event1: The Public Financial Management Act, 2016 (Act 921) modernizes and strengthens CAGD's legal framework.
ourHistory.era6Event2: Full transition to Electronic Fund Transfers — elimination of all manual cheque payments nationwide.
ourHistory.era6Event3: Controller & Accountant-General wins prestigious Governance and Civil Leader of the Year Award.
ourHistory.era6Event4: Ghana achieves its first Whole-of-Government Accounts — joining an elite group including the UK, Canada, and Australia.
ourHistory.era6Event5: TPRS upgraded with enhanced fraud prevention capabilities. 703 MDA spending units now covered.
ourHistory.era6Event6: 533 ghost employees detected and removed from government payroll; 14,027 National Service ghost names eliminated.
ourHistory.era6Event7: NIA-CAGD integration enables real-time biometric payroll validation through the Ghana Card.
ourHistory.whTimeline1Desc: The British colonial administration established 'The Treasury' in the Gold Coast to manage colonial finances and public revenue.
ourHistory.whTimeline2Desc: The Treasury was re-christened to the Accountant-General's Department, reflecting its expanded accounting and financial management responsibilities.
ourHistory.whTimeline3Desc: Under the newly independent Republic of Ghana, the department continued its critical mandate of safeguarding public finances.
ourHistory.whTimeline4Desc: Established under the Civil Service Act 1960 (CA. 5), CAGD was formally positioned as the Accounting Class of the Civil Service.
ourHistory.whTimeline5Desc: Renamed to the Controller & Accountant-General's Department, clearly defining its expanded role in exercising financial controls in budget execution.
ourHistory.whTimeline6Desc: The 1992 Constitution of Ghana formally enshrined CAGD's role and the CAG's responsibilities in public financial management.
ourHistory.whTimeline7Desc: The Public Financial Management Act, 2016 (Act 921) modernized and strengthened CAGD's legal framework for contemporary governance.
ourHistory.whTimeline8Desc: Eliminated all manual cheque payments nationwide, transitioning fully to Electronic Fund Transfers for all government payments.
ourHistory.whTimeline9Desc: Achieved Ghana's first comprehensive whole-of-government financial accounts — joining an elite group of nations including the UK, Canada, and Australia.
ourHistory.whTimeline10Desc: Real-time biometric payroll validation through integration with the National Identification Authority, eliminating ghost workers from the public payroll.
ourHistory.legalMandateIntro: The Controller and Accountant-General is enjoined by law to:

coreValues.title: Core Values
coreValues.description: Five guiding principles that shape how we serve Ghana's public financial management needs.
coreValues.clickToLearn: Click any value to learn how we put it into practice
coreValues.quote: Excellence is not a skill. It is an attitude — and at CAGD, it is our culture.
coreValues.fullDesc1: Every policy, system, and service we deliver is designed with the end user in mind — whether it's a government ministry awaiting fund releases, a civil servant checking their e-payslip, or a pensioner accessing their monthly payment. We measure our success by the satisfaction of those we serve.
coreValues.fullDesc2: With 16 regional offices spanning every corner of Ghana and coverage of 703 MDA spending units, we ensure no community is left behind. We swiftly respond to the needs and concerns of customers whether they're in Accra or the most remote district.
coreValues.fullDesc3: From detecting 533 ghost workers on government payroll to our commitment to adopting 31 of 36 IPSAS standards, integrity is the bedrock of everything we do. We will be true to our word and responsibilities and hold ourselves to the highest ethical standards in public financial management.
coreValues.fullDesc4: We invest in our people through Treasury Hour CPD sessions, TRELAS systems, the CAGD Welfare Scheme, and continuous professional development programs. A motivated, well-trained workforce is the engine of effective public financial management.
coreValues.fullDesc5: From transitioning to fully electronic payments in 2018 to integrating with the National Identification Authority for biometric payroll validation in 2024, we constantly push the boundaries of what's possible in government finance. Innovation isn't optional — it's how we stay relevant.

ourStructure.title: Our Structure
ourStructure.description: The Controller and Accountant-General is ably assisted by six Deputy Controllers and Accountant-Generals who head and manage six specialized divisions.
ourStructure.ourDivisionsDesc: Six specialized divisions delivering comprehensive public financial management across Ghana
ourStructure.divFaDesc: Leading, organizing, and controlling all resources needed for CAGD to deliver on its mandate — human, financial, physical and information.
ourStructure.divTreasuryDesc: Coordinating the receipts, custody, disbursement and transfer of public and trust monies as required by law.
ourStructure.divFmsDesc: Facilitating the development and implementation of efficient financial management systems for budget implementation and reporting.
ourStructure.divIctDesc: Creating and maintaining an integrated IT environment that advances the core mission of the Department.
ourStructure.divPayrollDesc: Development, implementation and review of payroll policies for all government employees and pensioners.
ourStructure.divAuditDesc: Independent appraisal of risk management and internal controls across all CAGD operations.
ourStructure.overviewPara1: The Controller and Accountant-General (CAG) is the Chief Accounting Officer of Government and the Chief Advisor to the Government of Ghana on accounting matters. The CAG offers advice on accounting matters to Government and is responsible to the Minister for Finance for the custody, safety and integrity of public funds.
ourStructure.overviewPara2: The CAG is ably assisted by six Deputy Controllers and Accountant-Generals who head and manage the affairs of the following Divisions. Together, these divisions cover all aspects of public financial management — from revenue collection and treasury operations to payroll processing, financial reporting, ICT systems, and internal audit.

leadershipPage.teamDesc: Our team consists of seasoned experts dedicated to serving the citizens of Ghana.

contactPage.title: Contact Us
contactPage.description: Get in touch with the Controller & Accountant-General's Department.
contactPage.name: Name
contactPage.subject: Subject
contactPage.successDesc: Thank you for contacting us. We will respond within 3-5 working days.
contactPage.quickFaqQ1: How do I access my e-Payslip?
contactPage.quickFaqA1: Visit epayslip.cagd.gov.gh with your staff ID and registered mobile number.
contactPage.quickFaqQ2: How do I report a payroll issue?
contactPage.quickFaqA2: Contact your regional CAGD office or email payroll@cagd.gov.gh with details.
contactPage.quickFaqA3: Monday to Friday, 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM. Closed on weekends and public holidays.

faqPage.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faqPage.description: Find answers to common questions about CAGD services, systems, and procedures.
faqPage.q1: How do I access my electronic payslip (e-Payslip)?
faqPage.a1: You can access your e-Payslip through the CAGD e-Payslip portal at epayslip.cagd.gov.gh. You will need your staff ID and registered mobile number to log in.
faqPage.a2: Salary delays may occur due to incomplete documentation, pending verification, or issues with your MDA's payroll submission. Contact your regional CAGD office for specific details.
faqPage.q3: How do I report a payroll discrepancy?
faqPage.a3: Report payroll discrepancies to your MDA's payroll unit first. If unresolved, contact the CAGD Payroll Division through your regional office with supporting documents.
faqPage.q4: How do I update my bank details for salary payment?
faqPage.a4: Submit a formal request through your MDA's HR/Payroll unit with a letter, valid ID, and new bank details. Changes are processed within 1-2 pay cycles.
faqPage.a5: The Ghana Integrated Financial Management Information System (GIFMIS) is the government's official financial management platform used for budget execution, payment processing, and accounting across all MDAs.
faqPage.q6: How do I get GIFMIS access?
faqPage.a6: GIFMIS access is granted through your MDA's Finance Officer. Submit a request with your job role and approval from your head of department.
faqPage.q7: What should I do if GIFMIS is down?
faqPage.a7: Contact the CAGD ICT Division helpdesk at ict@cagd.gov.gh or call the ICT support line. Planned maintenance windows are communicated in advance.
faqPage.q8: Where can I download CAGD reports?
faqPage.a8: Official reports are available on the Reports & Documents page of this website. You can filter by category and year, and download PDFs directly.
faqPage.q9: How often are financial reports published?
faqPage.a9: Annual reports are published yearly, while quarterly budget execution reports are released every three months. Special reports are published as needed.
faqPage.a10: International Public Sector Accounting Standards (IPSAS) are a set of accounting standards for use by public sector entities. Ghana is transitioning to IPSAS to improve transparency and comparability of financial reporting.
faqPage.q11: How many IPSAS standards has Ghana adopted?
faqPage.a11: Ghana has adopted 31 out of 36 IPSAS standards. The remaining 5 are in various stages of implementation. Check the IPSAS Implementation page for details.
faqPage.a12: CAGD offices operate Monday to Friday, 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM. Regional offices follow the same schedule.
faqPage.q13: How do I contact my regional CAGD office?
faqPage.a13: Visit the Contact Us page and click on your region to see the phone number, email, and address of the regional office.
faqPage.q14: Does CAGD offer internships or attachments?
faqPage.a14: Yes, CAGD accepts applications for student attachments and national service personnel. Submit your application letter and CV to the HR unit at the head office.

projectsPage.title: Our Projects
projectsPage.description: Driving reform and innovation in Ghana's public financial management through strategic projects that strengthen accountability and transparency.
projectsPage.transformingDesc: These strategic projects represent CAGD's commitment to building a modern, transparent, and accountable public financial management system for Ghana.

pfmrpPage.title: Public Financial Management Reform
pfmrpPage.description: A comprehensive initiative to modernize Ghana's public financial management systems through strengthened systems and procedures and targeted capacity building.
pfmrpPage.pfmrpTitle: The Public Financial Management Reform Project
pfmrpPage.pfmrpDesc: Eight institutional sub-components covering the key PFM systems and their implementing MDAs.
pfmrpPage.projectComponentsDesc: Four strategic components with 16 sub-components driving comprehensive reform across Ghana's public financial management landscape. Click each sub-component to view objectives and activities.
pfmrpPage.aboutDesc1: The Public Financial Management Reform Project (PFMRP) seeks to achieve improvement in budget management, financial control and reporting of the Government of Ghana with the aim of enhancing fiscal discipline, strategic allocation of resources and service delivery efficiency, through strengthened systems and procedures and targeted capacity building.
pfmrpPage.aboutDesc2: The Project has 4 components which are further divided into eight institutional sub-components covering the key PFM systems. Led by the Controller & Accountant-General's Department (CAGD), the reform has connected over 700 Ministries, Departments, and Agencies (MDAs) to centralized financial management systems.
pfmrpPage.relatedDocumentsDesc: Key manuals, accounting frameworks, and Chart of Accounts editions supporting the PFMRP reform.
pfmrpPage.quote: Enhancing budget credibility through transparent, accountable, and efficient public financial management.

ipsasPage.title: IPSAS Implementation
ipsasPage.description: Ghana's transition to International Public Sector Accounting Standards — improving transparency, comparability, and accountability in government financial reporting.
ipsasPage.benefitsDesc: Key advantages of adopting International Public Sector Accounting Standards.
ipsasPage.journeyDesc: Key milestones in coordinating IPSAS implementation across all covered entities.
ipsasPage.trackerDesc: Track Ghana's progress across all 36 IPSAS standards. Click any card to learn more.
ipsasPage.adoptionJourneyDesc: Ghana's progressive adoption of IPSAS standards over the past decade.
ipsasPage.resourcesDesc: Templates, policies, guidelines, and Chart of Accounts editions supporting IPSAS implementation.

divisions.clickOrgToView: Click the organogram to view full size
divisions.expandDirectorates: Expand each directorate to explore its sections and responsibilities.

fa.heroDesc: Leading, organizing and controlling all resources for CAGD to achieve its objectives efficiently and effectively.
fa.purposeDesc: The Finance and Administration Division (F&A) is responsible for leading, organizing, planning, formulating and controlling the resources of the Controller and Accountant-General's Department to achieve its objectives in an effective and efficient manner. The Division provides the Human, Financial, Physical and Information resources for the Department by facilitating the development of structures, setting up systems and designing business processes.
fa.orgDesc: The Finance & Administration Division is organised into five directorates, each with specialised sections delivering on CAGD's mandate.
fa.directoratesDesc: Five specialised directorates work together to ensure the efficient management of CAGD's human, financial, and physical resources.
fa.quote: The backbone of every great institution is its people and the systems that support them.
fa.keyFunctionsDesc: Core functions delivered by the Finance & Administration Division in support of CAGD's mandate.
fa.dir1Desc: Manages the department's internal financial resources including budgeting, expenditure control, and financial reporting.
fa.dir2Desc: Handles recruitment, staff welfare, performance management, and all administrative functions across 16 regions.
fa.dir3Desc: Policy, Planning, Budgeting, Monitoring & Evaluation, and Risk Management — ensuring CAGD's strategic goals are tracked and risks mitigated.
fa.dir4Desc: Manages all procurement activities and contract administration in compliance with the Public Procurement Act.
fa.dir5Desc: Designs and delivers professional development programs including the Treasury Hour CPD series and staff capacity building.
fa.kf1Desc: Recruitment, deployment, and professional development of CAGD staff across all regions.
fa.kf2Desc: Internal budgeting, expenditure management, and financial resource planning.
fa.kf3Desc: Strategic planning, policy formulation, and performance monitoring across the department.
fa.kf4Desc: Identifying, assessing, and mitigating organizational risks to ensure operational continuity.
fa.kf5Desc: Managing procurement and contract administration in compliance with public procurement laws.
fa.kf6Desc: Designing and delivering professional development programs and capacity building initiatives.

treasury.heroDesc: Control, measurement, analysis and classification of all government financial flows.
treasury.purposeDesc: The Treasury Division is responsible for coordinating the receipts, custody, disbursement and transfer of public and trust monies as required by law, relating each department's released budget to the Appropriation, and responsible for the day to day cash management of the Consolidated Fund and other funds.
treasury.orgDesc: The Treasury Division is organised into four directorates responsible for managing the nation's financial flows.
treasury.directoratesDesc: Four directorates work together to manage government receipts, disbursements, debt, and treasury coordination nationwide.
treasury.keyResponsibilitiesDesc: The Treasury Division carries out the following mandated responsibilities in public financial administration.
treasury.quote: Ensuring liquidity, accountability, and fiscal discipline in every government transaction.
treasury.keyFunctionsDesc: Core functions delivered by the Treasury Division in managing Ghana's public financial flows.
treasury.dir1Desc: Manages the central treasury operations including government receipts, payments, cash flow, and the Consolidated Fund.
treasury.dir2Desc: Oversees the collection, classification, and reporting of both tax and non-tax government revenues.
treasury.dir3Desc: Manages government debt portfolio, investment strategies, and public debt servicing for sustainable fiscal management.
treasury.dir4Desc: Coordinates treasury operations across 16 regional offices, foreign missions, and all MDAs/MMDAs nationwide.
treasury.kf1Desc: Day-to-day management of the Consolidated Fund and government cash resources.
treasury.kf2Desc: Ensuring proper collection and classification of all government revenues.
treasury.kf3Desc: Relating each department's released budget to the Appropriation Act.
treasury.kf4Desc: Managing relationships with Bank of Ghana and other financial institutions.
treasury.kf5Desc: Managing government debt portfolio and investment strategies.
treasury.kf6Desc: Preparing monthly, quarterly and annual treasury reports.
treasury.resp1: Control of liquidity in public financial administration
treasury.resp2: Assessment and advice on the efficiency of all aspects of the financial administration and financial assets of Government
treasury.resp3: Development and recommendation of suitable systems for management and control of the commitments and contingent liabilities of Government
treasury.resp4: Development of appropriate strategies and provision of effective advice and reporting systems to vote controllers
treasury.resp5: Evaluates and communicates to the CAG all updates relating to the operation of the government budget
treasury.resp6: Ensures the establishment of proper and necessary banking relationships with Bank of Ghana
treasury.resp7: Ensures proper collection of government revenues
treasury.resp8: Handles local government accounting and reporting requirements
treasury.resp9: Evolves financial systems for government fund management
treasury.resp10: Prepares monthly, quarterly and annual reports
treasury.resp11: Performs other duties as assigned by the Controller and Accountant-General

fms.heroDesc: Efficient financial management systems for budget implementation, accounting and reporting on Public funds.
fms.purposeDesc1: The Financial Management Services (FMS) Division is responsible for facilitating the development and implementation of efficient/sound Financial Management systems for budget implementation, accounting and reporting on Public funds.
fms.purposeDesc2: From producing consolidated national accounts to managing the Government of Ghana Chart of Accounts, facilitating GIFMIS operations, and driving public financial management reforms, the FMS Division provides the analytical and policy backbone that underpins Ghana's public financial management architecture.
fms.orgDesc: The FMS Division is organized into three directorates, each with specialized sections delivering on the Division's mandate.
fms.directoratesDesc: Three specialized directorates work in concert to deliver sound financial management across the public sector.
fms.quote: Sound financial management systems are the foundation of transparent and accountable governance.
fms.keyFunctionsDesc: The core responsibilities that define the FMS Division's contribution to Ghana's public financial management.
fms.dir1Desc: Responsible for facilitation of the usage of the Ghana Integrated Financial Management Information System (GIFMIS) by covered entities, management of standing imprest for covered entities, management of loans and advances granted to GoG staff, and management of 3rd party deposits for payroll deduction codes.
fms.dir2Desc: Core functions include the monitoring of revenue performance and reporting at covered entities, and monitoring compliance to standards and financial policies of Government by covered entities.
fms.dir3Desc: Explores best practices and provides suggestions and recommendations for enhancing the performance of the CAGD. Seeks to support CAGD's management in the development, implementation and review of laws, standards, accounting manuals, instructions and other technical documentation.
fms.kf1Desc: Production and consolidation of national accounts across central and local government.
fms.kf2Desc: Oversight and management of the Government of Ghana Chart of Accounts.
fms.kf3Desc: Facilitating the usage of Ghana's Integrated Financial Management Information System.
fms.kf4Desc: Monitoring revenue performance and financial reporting compliance across MDAs.
fms.kf5Desc: Development and review of accounting laws, standards, manuals and instructions.
fms.kf6Desc: Leading and managing public financial management reform initiatives.

ict.heroDesc: Creating and maintaining an integrated IT environment that advances the core mission of the Department.
ict.purposeDesc1: The ICTM Division exists to provide an integrated Information Technology environment that advances the core mission of the Department, and ensures the provision, maintenance and upgrade of appropriate Information Communication Technology (ICT) resources and services to support the operations of the Department.
ict.purposeDesc2: It also advises management on new developments in ICT and designs and implements ICT disaster recovery plans. From infrastructure management and cybersecurity to application support and data analytics, the Division ensures CAGD's technology ecosystem is resilient, secure, and fit for purpose.
ict.orgDesc: The ICTM Division is organized into three directorates that collectively manage the Department's technology landscape.
ict.directoratesDesc: Three directorates drive the Division's mission to deliver a secure, integrated and modern technology environment.
ict.quote: Technology is the bridge between efficient governance and accessible public services.
ict.keyFunctionsDesc: The core responsibilities that define the ICTM Division's contribution to CAGD's digital transformation and operational resilience.
ict.dir1Desc: Manages all ICT infrastructure including networks, servers, data centres, and ensures security compliance across CAGD systems.
ict.dir2Desc: Oversees ICT service management, controls, disaster recovery planning, and provides help desk support across the department.
ict.dir3Desc: Manages all business applications, system interfaces, data analysis capabilities, and provides technical maintenance and support for CAGD's digital platforms.
ict.kf1Desc: Advising management on new developments in ICT and technology strategy.
ict.kf2Desc: Provision, maintenance and upgrade of ICT infrastructure and networks.
ict.kf3Desc: Ensuring ICT security, compliance, and data protection across all CAGD systems.
ict.kf4Desc: Designing and implementing ICT disaster recovery and business continuity plans.
ict.kf5Desc: Managing and maintaining business applications including GIFMIS, TPRS, and e-Payslip.
ict.kf6Desc: Leveraging data analysis capabilities to support evidence-based decision making.

payroll.heroDesc: Development, implementation and review of policies and guidelines for processing GoG payroll.
payroll.purposeDesc: The Government Payroll Management Division is responsible for development, implementation and review of policies and guidelines for processing GoG payroll which includes payment and accounting for salaries and pensions, assignment of deduction codes for third party transactions.
payroll.additionalDesc: In addition to the main function of running the national payroll, other activities include:
payroll.orgDesc: Click the organogram to view in full size
payroll.directoratesDesc: Expand each directorate to explore its sections and responsibilities.
payroll.quote: Every government worker deserves timely, accurate, and transparent compensation.
payroll.keyFunctionsDesc: Core operational functions carried out by the Payroll Management Division.
payroll.dir1Desc: Processes monthly salaries for all active government employees nationwide. Manages payroll coordination, functional processing, statistics and reporting, third party deduction management, and regional salary disbursement.
payroll.dir2Desc: Manages all pension-related payroll functions including pension computation, processing, and monthly payment disbursement to retired government workers.
payroll.kf1Desc: Monthly processing and disbursement of salaries for all active government employees.
payroll.kf2Desc: Processing and disbursement of monthly pension payments to retired government workers.
payroll.kf3Desc: Processing payroll deductions for taxes, social security, unions, and third party obligations.
payroll.kf4Desc: Generation and transmission of payment vouchers for covered entity validation.
payroll.kf5Desc: Generating payroll statistics, analytics, and management reports.
payroll.kf6Desc: Detection and recovery of overpayments and unauthorized payments.
payroll.activity1: Determination of the means of transmitting employee compensation
payroll.activity2: Publishing dates for payment of salaries and pensions
payroll.activity3: Generation and transmission of monthly salary payment vouchers for validation by covered entities before final payroll run
payroll.activity4: Processing and accounting for payroll deductions to third parties
payroll.activity5: Recovery of overpayments and unauthorized payments from government employees

audit.heroDesc: Independent appraisal of risk management and internal controls across CAGD operations.
audit.purposeDesc: The Division is responsible for, among others, the execution of the internal audit function and other investigative activities within the CAGD. The Internal Audit Unit was established in accordance with the provisions of the Internal Audit Agency Act, 2003 (Act 658) and the Public Financial Management Act, 2016 (Act 921). The Division provides Management and the Audit Committee with assurance through an independent appraisal of the business processes and controls put in place to manage risks to enable the CAGD to achieve its objectives.
audit.legalDesc: The Division operates under a robust legislative framework that establishes its mandate and authority.
audit.legalCharter: The general mandate governing the Division's operations is covered by an Internal Audit Charter which outlines the objectives, structure, authority and the responsibilities of management.
audit.orgDesc: Click the organogram to view in full size
audit.directoratesDesc: Expand each directorate to explore its sections and responsibilities.
audit.quote: Vigilance and integrity — the twin pillars of public financial accountability.
audit.keyFunctionsDesc: Core operational functions carried out by the Audit & Investigation Division.
audit.law1Desc: Establishes the legal basis for internal audit functions in all government entities.
audit.law2Desc: Modernizes and strengthens the audit and investigation framework.
audit.law3Desc: Sets out the broad policy, objectives, structure, authority and responsibilities of management in respect of Internal Auditing.
audit.dir1Desc: Audits the active payroll and pensions payroll systems to detect irregularities, ghost workers, unauthorized payments, and ensure compliance with payroll policies and regulations.
audit.dir2Desc: Reviews financial statements and monitors the implementation of audit recommendations to ensure financial integrity and accountability across CAGD operations.
audit.dir3Desc: Conducts systems audits, IT audits, and investigations into financial irregularities, fraud, and other matters requiring forensic examination.
audit.kf1Desc: Independent appraisal of business processes, controls, and risk management systems.
audit.kf2Desc: Detecting and preventing fraudulent activities including ghost workers and unauthorized payments.
audit.kf3Desc: Specialized auditing of government payroll and pension payment systems.
audit.kf4Desc: Reviewing financial statements and ensuring compliance with accounting standards.
audit.kf5Desc: Auditing information technology systems and cybersecurity controls.
audit.kf6Desc: Conducting investigations into financial irregularities and suspected fraud.
